crossEventQuestion: When should AI infrastructure scale, and when should selected early-stage capacity projects be slowed or paused?
observation: The 2023 and 2024 events scale infrastructure under visible demand and capacity urgency, while the 2025 event slows or pauses selected early-stage projects when updated demand, adoption, and business-priority signals change the required timing or location.
invariant: Keep the AI capacity strategy demand-led and preserve flexibility by changing the most reversible asset commitments first.
boundaryConditions: Scale when customer demand, capacity urgency, and infrastructure economics remain supportable.
Pace or pause early-stage projects when location, timing, adoption, or business-priority signals weaken.
unresolvedConflicts: No event-specific Amy Hood statement explains the 2025 pause; the action is attributed to Noelle Walsh and linked to Amy Hood's earlier demand-management policy.

decisionAxis.decisionQuestion: Should infrastructure scale within operating-cost constraints or should selected early-stage projects be paced?

conditions: Customer demand and material AI capacity requirements remain observable.
Early-stage assets remain reversible and updated demand, adoption, or business-priority signals no longer support the original timing or location.

conditionDelta: Visible demand and urgent capacity needs justify scaling; weaker project-specific timing or location signals justify selective early-stage pacing.
actionDelta: Move from scaling capacity under cost discipline to slowing or pausing reversible early-stage projects while retaining the broader AI strategy.

review.rationale: User-approved PoC opposite event reviewed on the AI-capacity axis; Noelle Walsh event authorship and absence of an event-specific Amy Hood explanation remain explicit.
